title: merge=beads git driver invokes legacy 'bd merge' and silently drops issues
description: Repro: merging origin/master into a branch where both sides changed .beads/issues.jsonl produced a 104-line result missing 4 upstream issues (ogent-4c2, ogent-kid, ogent-pag, ogent-t3b); expected union was 108. .gitattributes maps .beads/issues.jsonl to merge=beads, and .git config defines merge.beads.driver = 'bd merge %A %O %A %B' — the legacy bd binary post-migration; the failure is silent (git keeps ours). Recovery that worked: git show BASE/THEIRS > .beads/beads.base.jsonl / .beads/issues.jsonl, then 'br sync --merge' (three-way base+DB+JSONL, verified 108 issues). Fix: repoint the driver at a br-based merge or drop the attribute so conflicts surface.
close_reason: Dropped the merge=beads attribute from .gitattributes (bead's stated alternative; br has no merge subcommand to repoint at). git check-attr now reports merge: unspecified, so divergent JSONL merges surface as normal conflicts instead of silently dropping issues via legacy bd. Recovery documented inline: git show base/theirs + br sync --merge.

title: Tool safety & audit-trail integrity
description: Convergent finding across the architecture, gptel, and llm-capability audits: the live tool path does not honor ogent's own approval policy or proof ledger, and a parallel FSM/approval/render stack sits unwired. Highest-risk cluster in the audit.
close_reason: All children complete: confirm bridge to gptel (fk6.1, P0), ledger recording in live paths (fk6.2), orphaned FSM stack excised + ogent-ui canonical (fk6.3), MCP tools gated by default effects (fk6.4), legacy fallback re-execution fixed (fk6.5). The live tool stack is now the only stack, honors approval policy on every path, and records a complete audit trail.

title: Forward :confirm and an :effects-derived confirm flag to gptel-make-tool
description: P0 (verified). ogent-register-tools (ogent-models.el:261-272) forwards only :category/:async/:include to gptel-make-tool, silently dropping :confirm. The bash/write-file/edit-file tools declare :confirm t and :effects risk critical/high (ogent-tools.el:801-832), but the live path binds gptel-tools/gptel-use-tools (ogent-ui.el:1996-1997) so gptel executes them natively. Verified against the shipped gptel (build-30.1): gptel-make-tool HAS a confirm slot and gptel--display-tool-calls prompts on it, so the control exists and ogent simply fails to set it. Net effect: a critical-risk shell tool runs with zero confirmation. Fix: forward :confirm, and derive confirm=t from any :effects entry whose :risk is high/critical. Add a regression test asserting registered gptel tools carry confirm.
close_reason: ogent-register-tools now passes :confirm to gptel-make-tool, derived from explicit :confirm OR :effects meeting the approval threshold (ogent-tool-spec-confirm-p). Verified against gptel build-30.1 (confirm slot + gptel--display-tool-calls prompt). 5 new tests incl. the P0 regression; risky bash/write-file/edit-file now resolve confirm=t. Routing this through ogent's own effects-aware prompt instead of gptel's is tracked separately in ogent-fk6.3.

title: Record ogent-ledger tool start/finish in every live execution path
description: P1 (verified). Tool events never reach the proof ledger that architecture.org 'Proof Ledger' promises. The only ledger-recording code is in ogent-tool-fsm.el:114-149, which is unreachable from production (FSM called only from its tests). Live executors (ogent-ui--execute-tool ~ogent-ui.el:1888-1899, the async streaming path ~1788-1808, gptel-native handling) record nothing; requests are logged but every tool execution is omitted, breaking the replayable audit trail. Fix: record start/finish (with declared effects, result hash, error, duration) in all three live paths.
close_reason: Tool start/finish now recorded to the proof ledger in all live execution paths: ogent-ui--execute-tool (sync + async-fallback) records start+finish with result hash, error, duration, and declared effects; ogent-ui--execute-tool-async wraps the streaming callback to record finish on done/error; the gptel-native tool-result handler records a finish per result. Self-guards via ogent-ledger-enabled. 3 new tests.

title: Unify the tool approval/render stack: wire FSM+approval+render into the live callback or excise them
description: P2 (verified). Two divergent stacks coexist. Live: ogent-ui.el approval via allow-list + read-char-choice + session deny list (~137-272, 1855-1886) and :TOOL: drawers (~2138-2181). Orphaned: ogent-tool-fsm.el, ogent-tool-approval.el (transient + session cache), ogent-tool-render.el (:TOOL_CALL_* drawers, nav minor mode) are called only from their own tests, and the FSM string-response parser is a stub returning nil. Decide one stack: wire the FSM/approval/render modules into the live pipeline, or delete them and update architecture.org. Depends on the :confirm fix landing first.
close_reason: Excised the orphaned FSM tool stack (ogent-tool-fsm/approval/render + 3 test suites). The live ogent-ui stack is now canonical and the only stack. Rehomed the two debug commands that depended on it: ogent-debug-replay-tool runs through ogent-ui--execute-tool; ogent-debug-show-approval-status reports the live allow-list/deny-list. Wired ogent-ui--execute-tool to populate debug tool-history (was only ever fed by the orphaned FSM, so replay now works for the first time). Updated architecture.org + README/ROADMAP/cabinet-org-os/tools.org/debugging.org to describe the live stack. Retargeted ledger + debug tests. 2402 tests green, lint clean.

title: Declare :effects on MCP-registered tools so they pass through the approval policy
description: P2. MCP-registered tools carry no :effects, so the effects-based approval gate cannot classify them and they bypass the risk policy entirely. Attach conservative default effects (e.g. network/unknown/high) to MCP tools at registration, or require an explicit effects mapping.
close_reason: MCP-registered tools now declare default :effects (ogent-mcp-default-tool-effects = network/external/high, tunable). network is an approval-kind and high meets the risk threshold, so MCP tools are gated through ogent approval and forwarded a confirm flag to gptel instead of auto-executing. 1 new test.

title: Fix legacy tool fallback re-executing tool calls on every streamed chunk
description: P1 (gptel + llm-capability audits). On the legacy :tool-use fallback path the streaming callback re-runs already-executed tool calls on each chunk after a tool round, and closes the request when gptel delivers a (tool-result ...) payload, dropping the continuation. Guard executed calls by id and stop treating tool-result as request completion.
close_reason: Legacy :tool-use fallback now guards on the payload's eq-identity (new handled-tool-use request slot), so each tool round dispatches exactly once instead of re-executing on every streamed chunk. Separately, (tool-call ...)/(tool-result ...) conses are excluded from the completion-close branch so a tool result no longer drops the model's continuation. 2 new regression tests.

title: Wire the analytics eval loop: record completions in production

The analytics module exists but nothing ever records a completion in the live path, so rate-up/down and the dashboard operate on no data. close_reason: Wired the analytics eval loop into the live request lifecycle: ogent-ui-register-request marks start, ogent-ui--append-response marks first-token, and ogent-ui--close-response records a completion (model/prompt/response, with TTFT + latency) on success only. Self-guards on ogent-analytics-enabled; wrapped in condition-case so analytics can never break request close. This was previously fully dark (setup never called, record-completion zero production callers) - now record + manual rate (C-c . +/-) + dashboard operate on real data. 3 new tests.

title: Add a native gptel agentic runner for Armory agents alongside the CLI adapters

title: MCP streamable-HTTP: incremental SSE, server GET streams, session lifecycle
description: Follow-up to ogent-wfv.5 (deliberately out of v1 scope, documented in ogent-mcp.el Commentary). The shipped HTTP transport is POST/JSON-response only: url-retrieve dispatches on completion, so a server that answers a POST with a long-lived text/event-stream would never deliver its JSON-RPC response (request stalls until timeout). Needed for full streamable-HTTP conformance: incremental SSE parsing (url-retrieve is unsuitable; needs a filter-based approach e.g. plz or raw network process), server-initiated GET event streams, and DELETE-on-disconnect session teardown.
close_reason: Streamable-HTTP conformance completed via curl subprocess (url.el cannot stream): incremental SSE frame parser (mid-line chunk splits, CRLF/lone-CR, multi-data: joins, heartbeat/comment skip, 1xx interim headers), POST content-type sniffing with JSON path preserved, optional server GET event stream (405 silent), DELETE session teardown + stream kill on disconnect, url-retrieve fallback when curl absent. 146/146 mcp suite on Emacs 29.1 and 30.2; byte-compile clean.

title: Test isolation: the suite can never write to real user stores
description: WHY (incident-driven): on 2026-07-13 a transiently-failing test inserted a bogus completion row into the developer's real .ogent-analytics.db (row 6638, cleaned up manually under approval); inspection showed the DB already contained thousands of historical test-traffic rows. The same hazard class covers the ledger store, org-capture targets, and the companion link registry (~/.emacs.d/ogent-companion-links.el) - any defcustom path that resolves to a real location during ert. Root cause: test isolation is per-suite discipline (cl-letf stubs) instead of a helper-level invariant. GOAL: test/ogent-test-helper.el makes non-isolation impossible by default; suites that genuinely need a store use an explicit opt-in fixture that provisions an ephemeral one. A tripwire converts violations into loud, named failures. PRINCIPLE: tests must also not create real files/dirs outside sanctioned temp fixtures (standing project rule).

title: Helper-level store guard: persistence off by default under ert
description: test/ogent-test-helper.el: make isolation a helper-level invariant, not per-suite discipline. (1) TIMING: the helper is loaded FIRST by every test file, but modules may load later AND defcustom defaults evaluate at module load - therefore the helper must FORCIBLY setq-default every persistence path/flag (not rely on load order), and must ALSO re-assert them via an ert setup hook so a module loaded mid-suite cannot restore its default. (2) SCOPE: setq-default ogent-analytics-enabled nil, ogent-ledger-enabled nil; point ogent-companion-link-registry-file, ogent-capture-notes-file, ogent-capture-companion-file, ogent-analytics-db-name at values under a per-session temp root that is NEVER auto-created (creation only via the opt-in fixture). AUDIT lisp/ for every defcustom/defconst whose default resolves under user-emacs-directory, org-directory, HOME, or the project root (grep expand-file-name over those bases) and add each to the guard list with a checked comment naming it - the audit list is the deliverable, not just the known four. (3) SUBPROCESSES: sqlite mutations bypass write-region entirely (native sqlite API), and spawned processes inherit the environment - document that the tripwire bead guards sqlite open/execute at the chokepoints, and that the fixture macro exports OGENT_TEST_STORE_ROOT into process-environment so any future subprocess-based store code can honor it. (4) FIXTURE: (ogent-test-with-real-store KIND &rest body) provisions an ephemeral store under the temp root and re-enables the subsystem within body only; nesting allowed; always cleans state (not files) on exit. TESTS: meta-tests asserting defaults are off/redirected after loading the helper; a late (require 'ogent-analytics) does not resurrect persistence; fixture round-trip (enabled inside, disabled after).
notes: FIXTURE FILE POLICY (standing no-deletion rule): fixtures never delete files - not even ones they create. Preference order: (1) in-memory sqlite (Emacs sqlite-open with nil filename) for analytics fixtures - zero filesystem footprint, the default for KIND 'analytics; (2) checked-in read-only fixtures under test/data/; (3) unavoidable file-backed fixtures go under the OS temp root via make-temp-file and are RETAINED (OS lifecycle owns them), with the path logged so a human can inspect; cleanup on exit resets Lisp STATE only (hooks, variables, connections closed), never the filesystem.
close_reason: Helper store guard shipped: forced setq-default over 3 flags + 12 audited paths (audit found real hazards beyond the bead's four: session dir, BOTH OAuth token paths incl. the module's resolve-then-write probing, codemap cache, snippets, agenda file), ert-run-test :before advice re-assertion, ogent-test-with-real-store fixture (in-memory sqlite for analytics, retained logged dirs for file kinds, OGENT_TEST_STORE_ROOT exported). 8/8 meta-tests; analytics 59/59; companion 44/44 after binding persist-links in 3 tests (2 also converted to zero-FS fixtures). Guard spec exposed as data for the aq8.2 tripwire.

title: Tripwire: fail any test that touches a real store path
description: The tripwire converts isolation violations into loud named failures. File-write advice alone MISSES the incident class: Emacs's native sqlite API mutates DBs without write-region, and subprocesses can write anywhere. Guard the actual chokepoints, in the helper, active only under noninteractive ert: (1) sqlite: advise sqlite-open (and ogent-analytics--get-db) to fail when the DB path is outside the sanctioned temp roots; (2) store writers: ogent-ledger-record's file writer, ogent-companion--write-link-registry, org-capture target resolution; (3) processes: advise make-process/start-process to fail when any :command argument or default-directory references a sanctioned-real-store path (narrow on the store paths, NOT a blanket subprocess ban - armory fixtures legitimately spawn nothing today but adapters stub make-process); (4) allowlist: armory suites' make-temp-file roots register via the fixture; every allowlist entry needs a justification comment. LOGGING: violation message 'TEST <ert-current-test> attempted real-store access: <path> via <fn>' - CI logs must pinpoint the leak instantly. TESTS: a deliberately-violating probe per chokepoint (expected-result :failed) proving each fires; compliant fixture tests proving silence; the hash-audit bead (aq8.4) remains the backstop for anything these chokepoints miss.
close_reason: Tripwire armed at the real chokepoints (batch-ert only): sqlite-open + ogent-analytics--get-db, ledger append, companion registry writer, org-capture-expand-file, make-process/start-process (store-path-narrowed, not blanket). Violations fail loudly naming test+path+fn. 24 probe/meta tests green (deliberate violations expected-fail per chokepoint); analytics 59/59 stays green. Guard spec consumed as data from aq8.1.

title: Multi-model fan-out: one prompt, N models, side-by-side responses
description: WHY: AGENTS.md names 'multi-model fan-out' as a core scenario the test fixtures under test/data/ were built to exercise - it is the one founding promise never shipped. Every primitive now exists: the request engine keys concurrent requests by id (ogent-ui--request-table), streaming callbacks are per-request, the model registry+picker handle model sets and roles, failover (wfv.7) proved re-dispatch works, and analytics records per-model completions (fan-out makes those rows born-comparable: same prompt+context, different model). DESIGN SKETCH: ogent-fanout sends the identical prompt+context to N registry models; each response streams under its own '*** Response (<model>)' sibling headline beneath ONE request block; the block records a fan-out group id; per-member status chips in the block header; a group-abort action; analytics rows tagged with the group id for later comparison. UX DECISIONS RECORDED: default model set = a defcustom list falling back to the picker's role models; member failure does NOT trigger provider failover into a model already in the group (that would duplicate a sibling) - failover is disabled within fan-out members, failures render as failed chips. NON-GOALS (v1): no automatic winner selection; compare/merge UX is the follow-up bead.

title: Fan-out core: group orchestration through the request engine
description: lisp/ui/ogent-ui-send.el + engine: ogent-fanout (autoloaded, interactive) builds prompt+context ONCE (identical ogent-context-build-with-source result shared across members - context must not be recomputed per model, both for cost and comparability), then issues N requests via the existing send path with :fanout-group <uuid> in each request's context plist. Response layout: one Request block; each member creates its own Response sibling headline at prepare-response-block time (the engine already supports the headline convention; extend prepare to take an existing request block instead of creating one). Failover interplay: requests carrying :fanout-group skip ogent-ui--handle-provider-error's failover branch (classification still logs; the member just fails). TESTS: stubbed gptel with N interleaved streaming callbacks - chunks route to the correct sibling headline with zero cross-contamination (the critical invariant); group id present on every analytics record (fixture DB via the isolation fixture); single-member degenerate case behaves exactly like ogent-request.
notes: TRUTH AMENDMENT: analytics tagging is DORMANT in production - the engine passes :fanout-group only when the recorder's arity accepts it, and today's real ogent-analytics-record-completion is 4-arg, so no group reaches the DB yet. The live half moves to ogent-z0k.3 (schema migration bead): fanout_group column + recorder keyword param + end-to-end test with the REAL recorder. Buffer-side group id (OGENT_FANOUT_GROUP drawer) IS live.
close_reason: Fan-out core shipped: ogent-fanout builds context ONCE (call-count tested), N members share one Request block with per-member Response sibling headlines on markers (interleaved-stream isolation proven), OGENT_FANOUT_GROUP drawer property, failover disabled for members via nil :dispatch (classification still logs; control test proves non-fanout retry unchanged), analytics tagging via arity-sniffing forward-compat (real 4-arg recorder untouched), single-member case normalized-transcript-equal to ogent-request. 8/8 new suite + ui 176 + fallback 18 green.

title: Fan-out model-set selection UX
description: Selection sources in precedence order: explicit C-u prompt (completing-read-multiple over registry ids, aliases resolved - reuse picker canonicalization from bead 7sd), ogent-fanout-default-models defcustom, picker role set fallback. Refuse duplicate models (canonicalize first), refuse empty set with a helpful user-error naming the three sources. Registry-wire the command (Epic A pattern; coordinate keys with the discoverability epic to avoid chord collisions). TESTS: alias canonicalization, duplicate rejection, precedence order, empty-set error message content.
close_reason: Model-set UX shipped: canonicalization via ogent-models-resolve-designator (aliases + @roles), duplicate/unknown user-errors, precedence arg > selected-models > ogent-fanout-default-models (defcustom renamed to bead spec, clean cutover) > distinct role models, empty-set error naming all three sources, C-u completing-read-multiple. C-f chord row added + reservation unlocked. 18/18 send suite.

title: Fan-out lifecycle: status chips, group abort, watchdog
description: Request block header shows per-member chips: model id + status (streaming/done/failed/aborted) via ogent-ui--update-block-header extension - one header line, updated as members finish. ogent-fanout-abort (registry-wired) aborts every in-flight member of the group at point via the existing abort path; per-member watchdogs already exist (per-request) - verify group abort cancels them. Partial completion is a feature: done members stay, failed members show the classified error in their sibling headline body (same error block format as single requests). TESTS: chip transitions across interleaved completions; group abort leaves done members intact and marks in-flight ones aborted; watchdog timeout of one member does not disturb siblings; request-table drains to zero after group completion (no zombie rows - mirror the wfv.7 test pattern).
design: Add ogent-fanout-group-done-hook: run with (GROUP-ID RESULTS) when the LAST member reaches a terminal state (done/failed/aborted all count) - this is compare mode's trigger and the external-automation hook point. RESULTS is a list of (MODEL-ID STATUS . RESPONSE-MARKER). Hook errors are demoted (condition-case + message) - a user hook must never wedge group bookkeeping. Test: hook fires exactly once per group, after the final terminal transition, with accurate statuses; a throwing hook does not prevent table cleanup.
close_reason: Lifecycle shipped: group registry seeded with full member set BEFORE dispatch (no premature done), per-member chips in the shared header (multi-member only - single-member stays byte-identical to ogent-request), ogent-fanout-abort on C-k (group at point via inherited drawer property; done members untouched, watchdogs cancelled), ogent-fanout-group-done-hook fires exactly once with (GROUP-ID (MODEL-ID STATUS . MARKER)...) in dispatch order, errors demoted, tables drain to zero (leak-tested incl. throwing hook). 18/18 + ui 176 + keys 61.

title: Fan-out compare mode: pairwise diff and keep-this-one
description: Follow-up UX (sequenced after core lands): ogent-fanout-compare on a completed group offers pairwise ediff of two member responses (plain-text ediff-buffers of the rendered bodies); ogent-fanout-keep on a member marks the losing siblings with the org ARCHIVE tag (DECIDED: org-toggle-archive-tag - org-native, reversible, folds by default; NOT org-archive-subtree which moves text to another file, and never deletion). Winner gets no marking. Rating interplay: keeping a member records rating 5 for its completion when the analytics rating pipeline (eval-loop epic) has landed - soft integration, fboundp-guarded, losers left unrated (an unpicked response is not evidence of badness). TESTS: ediff invocation args (stub ediff-buffers); keep tags losers ARCHIVE and leaves winner intact; keep is reversible (re-running on another member swaps tags); analytics hook fires only when available.
notes: DECIDED in plan space: losers get the org ARCHIVE tag (org-toggle-archive-tag) - org-native, reversible, folds by default, respects the no-deletion posture. NOT subtree strike-through, NOT org-archive-subtree (which MOVES text to another file - too destructive for a compare gesture). Winner gets no marking.

title: Analytics eval loop: ratings, auto-outcomes, and cost tracking
description: WHY: bead wfv.3 wired completion RECORDING in production, but every row sits outcome='pending', rating=0 forever (verified in the real DB 2026-07-13: 6600+ rows, none rated) - the 'eval loop' has no loop. The schema already carries outcome/rating columns and ogent-analytics--pending-completion + ogent-analytics--update-completion exist; what is missing is (1) a frictionless rating gesture, (2) automatic outcome signals where ogent already knows the answer (edit accepted => success), (3) cost: tokens are recorded but never priced, so the dashboard cannot answer 'which model earns its cost'. GOAL: close the loop so the picker/dashboard can eventually rank models on evidence (see the picker-stats bead, which this epic unblocks). PRIVACY NOTE for future selves: everything stays in the local per-project sqlite DB; no telemetry.

title: One-key rating on the response headline
description: Registry-wired action (coordinate with Epic A for the chord) active on '*** Response' headlines: rate the completion 1-5. DECIDED: numeric 1-5 via read-char-choice after the chord (two keystrokes total; schema rating column is already integer and the dashboard distribution needs granularity - thumbs would collapse it). Resolution: the response headline must be traceable to its analytics completion row - the pending-completion var only covers the LAST request, so persist the completion id into the request block properties (OGENT_COMPLETION_ID) at record time; the rating action reads it from the drawer. Sets outcome='rated' alongside. Idempotent re-rating updates the row. TESTS (ephemeral DB fixture from the isolation epic): rating round-trip via the drawer id; re-rating updates not duplicates; rating without a completion id gives a helpful user-error naming the likely cause (analytics disabled at request time).
notes: DECIDED in plan space: numeric 1-5 rating (schema rating column is already an integer; the dashboard distribution view needs granularity; thumbs collapse to 5/1 and lose it). Gesture: registry chord then a single digit 1-5 read via read-char-choice - two keystrokes total. No transient needed.
close_reason: One-key rating shipped on the reserved * chord (row added, guard unlocked): ogent-analytics-rate-response reads OGENT_COMPLETION_ID from the drawer (inherit), read-char-choice 1-5 per DECIDED, outcome='rated', idempotent re-rate, helpful no-id error naming the disabled-analytics cause. Engine stamps the id at record time - plain requests on the Request drawer, fan-out members on their OWN Response headline (last-writer-win corruption avoided). analytics 75/75, keys 62/62.

title: Auto-outcome from edit acceptance signals
description: ogent-edit-resolved-hook (shipped in tdf.2) fires with accept/reject resolutions; set outcome='accepted'/'rejected' on the linked completion WITHOUT touching an explicit user rating (explicit rating always wins - record the precedence rule in a comment). TRACING CORRECTNESS: the org-drawer OGENT_COMPLETION_ID plumbing (rating bead) does NOT work here - quick-edit/diagnostic-repair run from ordinary code buffers with no org block. Instead: stamp the completion id into the pending-request plist at analytics-record time (ogent-edit--pending-request already threads :attempt/:tried through the fallback work, same pattern) and copy it onto each ogent-edit struct the response parse produces; the resolved-hook handler reads it off the struct. TESTS: accept sets outcome, reject sets outcome, explicit rating survives a later auto-outcome, edits without completion linkage are silently skipped (no error spam), multi-edit responses map every edit to the SAME completion id (one request = one completion).

title: Cost: per-model pricing table and computed completion cost
description:
ogent-analytics-model-pricing defcustom: alist model-pattern -> plist (:input-per-mtok :output-per-mtok USD). Patterns match registry ids with a longest-prefix rule (models get suffixed variants). Cost computed at record time from prompt/response token estimates and stored in a new completions.cost_usd column (sqlite ALTER TABLE migration - follow or establish the version-pragma idempotent-migration pattern in ogent-analytics--get-db; document it). Missing pricing => NULL cost, never 0 (0 lies; NULL renders as '-'). Ship a starter table for the shipped registry models with a dated comment. 

EXPANDED (moved from pje.1 per truth amendment): the same migration adds completions.fanout_group TEXT NULL, and ogent-analytics-record-completion gains an optional group parameter (trailing &rest-style keyword or explicit 5th optional - pick, document; the engine already passes :fanout-group when arity allows, see ogent-ui--analytics-completion-args). END-TO-END TEST REQUIRED: a stubbed-gptel fan-out through the REAL recorder into an in-memory DB yields N rows sharing the group id; plain requests yield NULL fanout_group. 

TESTS: migration idempotence (run twice on one in-memory connection), longest-prefix matching, NULL for unknown models, arithmetic spot checks, fanout-group column round-trip.
close_reason: Pricing/cost + EXPANDED scope shipped: version-pragma idempotent migration (v1: cost_usd, fanout_group, transactional rebuild widening CHECKs since SQLite can't alter constraints), longest-prefix pricing table dated 2026-07 covering all shipped ids, NULL-never-0 for unpriced, cost computed+stored at record time; recorder accepts :fanout-group via trailing kwargs (arity='many' satisfies the engine sniff); REAL-recorder e2e: stubbed-gptel fan-out -> in-memory DB -> N rows share group id, plain NULL. Migration twice-on-one-connection tested; legacy v0 data preserved.

title: Dashboard: cost and rating views + unrated nudge
description: ogent-analytics dashboard additions: cost by model (sum/avg) and by day, rating distribution per model, unrated-completion count with a hint naming the rating key. Keep the existing keys (g/e/o) working; extend the org/CSV exports with the new columns. TESTS: rendering against a fixture DB with known rows - assert the aggregate numbers literally (golden values), exports include new columns, empty-DB renders without error.

title: Eval-loop test pass: end-to-end with detailed logging
description: E2E ert scenario (ephemeral DB): record completion via a stubbed request -> drawer carries completion id -> rate it -> accept an edit linked to it -> assert final row state (rating, outcome precedence, cost) with a log line per transition ('completion 42: recorded -> rated 4 -> outcome accepted (rating wins)'). This is the living documentation of the loop's state machine; keep the log format stable.
notes: Fixture policy: use the in-memory sqlite fixture (aq8.1 KIND 'analytics) - the e2e scenario needs no file-backed DB. Same for z0k.1/z0k.3/z0k.4 test sections; the migration-idempotence test in z0k.3 can run the migration twice against one in-memory connection.

title: Doctor: full environment validator with remediation hints
description: WHY: ogent's runtime surface is now wide - gptel backends + two OAuth flows (tokens EXPIRE), curl (the SSE transport hard-degrades to non-streaming url-retrieve without it), optional packages (org-ql/vterm/markdown-mode), external agent CLIs (which the adapter layer now honestly marks unsupported when ungrounded, 8e0.7), MCP servers, per-project sqlite DBs, and a real observed trap: Emacs 29 cannot lazy-load .elc compiled by Emacs 30 (bit an agent on 2026-07-13; the test helper carries a private workaround recipe). Each of these is a category of confusing user breakage that a single M-x ogent-doctor pass/warn/fail report with one-line remediations would kill. CHASSIS: lisp/ogent-doctor.el exists; this epic normalizes it into a check framework and fills it. PRINCIPLE: checks are pure-read, fast (<2s total without opt-in network), individually testable with stubbed environments, and NEVER auto-fix (report + remediation text only; fixing is the user's call).

title: Doctor check framework: registry of pass/warn/fail checks
description: Normalize ogent-doctor.el into: a check registry (name, category, fn -> (status . detail), remediation string), a report buffer grouped by category with aligned status glyphs, and an ogent-doctor--run-checks core that catches per-check errors (a crashing check reports as fail with the error, never aborts the run). Existing checks migrate onto the registry. Exit contract for batch use: (ogent-doctor-batch) returns non-zero-style summary for CI/scripting. TESTS: registry check crash containment, report rendering with fixture results, category grouping.
close_reason: Doctor normalized into a check-registry framework: entries (id/label/category/fn/remediation/opt-in), zero-arg checks returning (STATUS . DETAIL), per-check crash containment (signal->error result, malformed->named error, run never aborts), grouped glyph-aligned report with remediation lines on warn/error only, ogent-doctor-batch with 0/1/2 exit contract, prefix-arg opt-in inclusion. 11 pre-existing checks migrated (enumerated in bead comments via agent report). 23/23 suite.

title: Doctor checks: auth, transports, optionals, CLIs, stores, elc staleness
description: Fill the registry (each check its own small fn, stubbed-env tests per check, no network by default): (1) gptel backend configured + API key/bearer present for the session default model; (2) Anthropic OAuth tokens.el expiry (warn <7d, fail expired; read-only file check); (3) Codex auth.json presence/mode; (4) curl on PATH - warn 'MCP HTTP falls back to non-streaming' when absent; (5) optionals org-ql/vterm/markdown-mode - info-level, name what each unlocks; (6) adapter CLIs: for each registered adapter with an executable, PATH check + surface :unsupported-reason for the honestly-unsupported ones (pi/grok) so users see WHY; (7) analytics DB integrity_check pragma (per-project, only when DB exists); (8) stale-elc trap: scan load-path ogent dirs for .elc whose stored Emacs major version exceeds the running one (parse .elc header bytes) - warn with the recompile remediation; (9) MCP servers (OPT-IN flag on the command, network): initialize handshake with 3s timeout per server. TESTS: one per check with cl-letf/file fixtures; the elc check gets a synthesized header fixture.
close_reason: All 9 checks landed, each own fn + stubbed-env tests, no network by default: default-model API key presence (secret never in detail), Anthropic tokens.el expiry (error expired / warn <7d), codex auth mode, curl with exact fallback wording, optionals org-ql/vterm/markdown-mode naming what each unlocks, adapter CLI PATH checks surfacing :unsupported-reason verbatim, analytics DB PRAGMA integrity_check (never creates the DB), stale-elc scanner (header-parsed Emacs major > running = warn with recompile remediation), MCP handshake probe (opt-in, 3s timeout, leaves live connections alone).

title: Doctor e2e: full run report golden test
description: Golden-file style test (testing-golden pattern): run the full doctor in a fully-stubbed environment and assert the rendered report matches a golden fixture (scrub machine-specific paths). Guards report format stability so remediation text and grouping do not silently regress. Detailed logging: on mismatch print a unified diff of the report.
close_reason: Full-run golden shipped: all 20 registered checks (incl. opt-in MCP) execute through the real runner against a fully-stubbed environment boundary; report compared whole against a blessed golden (machine paths scrubbed to placeholders), pure-elisp unified diff on drift, id-order assertion makes new registry entries fail by name until the golden is re-blessed. Failure path proven with a perturbed report. 24/24 doctor suite.

title: Native runner: live round streaming + native session resume
description: PARITY GAPS left open by wfv.6/8e0.6 (both closed with these explicitly reported): (1) STREAMING - native runs are silent until finalize while CLI runs show progressive output. DECIDED: stream each round's text into the conversation buffer ONLY when a live buffer is already visiting the conversation file (insert at the current turn's position, read-only-safe, marker-based); when no buffer visits it, no live streaming - the file stays finalize-written exactly as today (canonical-file invariant untouched; we never write the file per-chunk). (2) RESUME - gptel-native declares :supports-session-resume nil, but turns are already persisted in the conversation org: native resume reloads prior turns into the gptel message list and continues. DECIDED: resume starts a FRESH iteration budget (ogent-armory-native-max-iterations counts per run, not per conversation - a resumed conversation is a new run over old context); turn numbering continues from the stored turns. Flip the capability flag to t ONLY in the same commit as the working implementation + tests (the 8e0.6 honesty rule). TESTS: streaming chunks appear incrementally in a visiting buffer (stubbed gptel, temp conversation via the armory fixture macros) and do NOT touch the file until finalize; no-visiting-buffer case byte-identical to today; resume rebuilds the exact message list from a fixture conversation (golden list assertion), continues turn numbering, fresh iteration budget; capability flag matches behavior (registry test).
close_reason: Native/CLI parity closed per DECIDED semantics: round text streams into a buffer already visiting the conversation file (marker-based, read-only-safe, undo/modified-neutral, file NEVER written per-chunk - finalize-only, byte-identical no-visiting-buffer case proven by whole-tree comparison); native resume reloads stored turns into the message list (golden-list tested, fresh iteration budget, turn numbering continues); :supports-session-resume flipped nil->t in the same change (8e0.6 honesty rule). userlock supersession handled for the run-owned file. 21/21 native + 12/12 adapter.

title: bd projection: emit OGENT_ISSUE_PARENT from parent-child deps
description: Finishes the 8e0.4 story: the armory graph honors OGENT_ISSUE_PARENT on issue-link records but no automated writer emits it (explicitly descoped there - 'writer belongs to the bd Org projection'). ogent-issues-bd--org-projection already writes OGENT_BLOCKED_BY from :blocked_by; br issue JSON carries parent-child dependencies - extract the parent id (the depends_on_id of the parent-child dep entry; verify field name against br list --json for a child bead) and emit :OGENT_ISSUE_PARENT: when present. TESTS (ogent-issues-bd-tests projection suite): fixture child issue emits the property; parentless issues emit nothing; graph e2e - projection output fed through record-metadata yields :issue-parent.
close_reason: Projection emits :OGENT_ISSUE_PARENT: extracted via all three empirically-verified br JSON shapes (show :parent string / show deps entry id / JSONL depends_on_id; list --json carries no deps - documented). Graph e2e proves projection->record-metadata->:issue-parent. 103/103 issues-bd suite (+7).

title: Surface failover status in the request block header
description: wfv.7's retry/failover currently narrates only via *Messages*: the request block header should show the recovery state so users understand latency instead of suspecting a hang. Plumbing exists: ogent-ui--update-block-header writes status/latency already; the fallback dispatch closure knows attempt/tried state. DECIDED semantics: retry (same model) updates the SAME block's header in place - 'retrying (n/max) in <delay>s'; failover sets the old block's terminal status 'failed over -> <model-id>' and the re-dispatch creates its normal NEW request block (no cross-links: the blocks are adjacent siblings; a link is plumbing without navigation gain). TESTS: header text transitions under the stubbed-timer fallback tests (extend the wfv.7 suites in test/ui/ogent-ui-tests.el): transient error -> retrying text with correct n/max/delay; failover -> old block terminal text names the substitute model; success after retry clears to the normal done status format.
notes: DECIDED in plan space: on failover the OLD request block's header status becomes 'failed over -> <model-id>' (terminal state for that block) and the re-dispatch creates its normal NEW request block; no cross-links (blocks are adjacent siblings in the buffer; a link adds plumbing for no navigation gain). Retry (same model) updates the SAME block's header in place: 'retrying (n/max) in <delay>s'.
close_reason: DECIDED semantics shipped: retry rewrites same block header in place ('retrying (n/max) in Ns' with per-model budget/backoff), failover marks old block terminal 'failed over -> <id>' via recomputed candidate (handle-error contract untouched), no cross-links. :fallback-status carried in request context. ui suite 175/175(+1 skip), fallback 18/18.

title: Token-budget preview: estimated prompt size before send
description: Silent context truncation is the classic LLM-tooling betrayal. ogent already has a token estimator (ogent-analytics-estimate-tokens) and a context preview buffer: show estimated prompt tokens in (1) the context preview header and (2) the request confirmation path, with a warn threshold from the model registry's context-window property (add :context-window to registry entries for shipped models; unknown => no warning, never a fabricated limit). Estimator is heuristic - label it '~' and document the ±20% caveat in the docstring. TESTS: estimate rendering in preview (fixture context), threshold warning at boundary, unknown-window silence, registry property presence for all shipped models.
notes: Fan-out interplay: a fan-out send multiplies prompt cost by N members - when the request is a fan-out group, the preview/confirmation must show 'N x ~tokens' (per-member context is identical by design, see pje.1). Soft integration: read the group member count when present; zero coupling otherwise.
close_reason: Token-budget preview shipped: :context-window grounded+cited on all 16 shipped models (accessor returns nil for unknown - never fabricated), '~N tokens' in all three preview render paths' header lines + dispatch echo after validation, warn at estimate>=window (boundary-exact tests), fan-out shows 'N x ~tokens' with per-member window check per the bead's N-x note, unknown-window silent. models 49/49, ui 185, send 27/27.

title: Per-model request tuning: timeout/retry properties in the registry
description: Global ogent-ui-request-timeout + fallback retry customs mistreat heterogeneous models (slow local models vs fast APIs). Add optional registry properties :request-timeout, :max-retries, :retry-base-delay consumed by the watchdog and ogent-provider-handle-error, falling back to the existing globals (globals stay the documented default; properties are overrides - no behavior change for untouched configs). TESTS: watchdog uses the model override, fallback consults per-model retries, absent properties preserve current behavior byte-for-byte (regression pin).
close_reason: Per-model :request-timeout/:max-retries/:retry-base-delay registry overrides with plist-member semantics (explicit nil/0 honored), consumed by watchdog + handle-error + retry-delay; globals stay defaults, absent-property behavior regression-pinned byte-for-byte. models 47/47, fallback 18/18 incl. 4 new.

title: Model picker: evidence columns from analytics
description: Once the eval loop lands, the picker should show evidence at the decision point: median latency, completion count, mean rating, and (when priced) mean cost per completion, per model, from the per-project analytics DB. Graceful degradation: no DB or no rows => columns absent (never zeros). Width-aware like the 7sd header work. TESTS: fixture DB -> exact column values; empty DB -> columns absent; alias canonicalization maps stats onto picker rows correctly. DEPENDS on the eval-loop epic (rating/cost columns must exist).

title: Adapter capability grounding harness: versioned CLI help snapshots
description: 8e0.7's standing rule: never invent CLI flags; 8e0.6 left gemini/cursor/opencode resume claims nil for lack of grounding. Make grounding a data update: test/data/cli-help/<adapter>-<version>.txt snapshots (checked in, hand-collected), a comparison command (ogent-armory-adapter-ground) that diffs a live --help against the snapshot and reports drift, and builder unit tests that assert emitted flags appear verbatim in the snapshot. When someone finally runs gemini --help, wiring resume becomes: save snapshot, write builder, flip capability - with the harness enforcing the honesty rule mechanically. TESTS: drift detection on a doctored snapshot, builder-flag-in-snapshot assertions for codex/claude (their grounded flags today), missing-snapshot behavior (skip with named warning, not failure).
close_reason: Grounding harness shipped in ogent-armory-adapter.el: checked-in verbatim snapshots (codex-cli 0.142.0: three help surfaces incl. top-level for --ask-for-approval; claude-code 2.1.199) with provenance headers, byte-equality-proven capture path; ogent-armory-adapter-ground diffs live help vs version-matched snapshot (exact-version preference, version< fallback), C-u saves a new snapshot (the wire-a-new-adapter data-update step); missing snapshot = named warning skip. Builder flag assertions: every emitted codex/claude flag verbatim-grounded with boundary-aware matching. 18/18 adapter suite; live e2e zero drift on both CLIs.

title: Export conversation to kill-ring for external paste
description: docs promise shareability; the last step is manual (open export buffer, select, copy). ogent-export-conversation-to-kill-ring: run the ogent-md export (existing rails: climbs to conversation root, drawer stripping) straight into the kill-ring + system clipboard, message the size ('copied 2.1k chars as Markdown'). Registry-wire it (Epic A pattern - coordinate chord). TESTS: kill-ring content equals buffer-export content for the fixture conversation; message format; works from inside a Response child (root-climb reuse).
close_reason: ogent-export-conversation-to-kill-ring shipped on the reserved C-w chord (registry row added, reservation unlocked per protocol): reuses the conversation-root climb + ogent-md backend, kill-new to kill-ring/system clipboard, size message. 19/19 ox suite (+5), 61/61 keys.

title: Persisted fan-out tagging: end-to-end proof with the real recorder
description: Epic-side acceptance for the pje.1 promise that fan-out analytics rows are born comparable. The schema+recorder-param work lands in ogent-z0k.3 (fanout_group column, recorder group parameter - moved there by truth amendment since pje.1's arity-sniffing engine path is dormant against today's 4-arg recorder). THIS bead proves the promise end-to-end once z0k.3 lands: ert scenario driving a stubbed-gptel ogent-fanout through the REAL ogent-analytics-record-completion into an in-memory DB, asserting N rows share the group id, plain requests yield NULL, and the group id matches the buffer's OGENT_FANOUT_GROUP drawer property (buffer<->DB join key intact). Plus: remove the arity-sniff transitional shim in ogent-ui--analytics-completion-args if z0k.3's final signature makes it unnecessary (clean cutover), and note the tagging in docs/getting-started.md fan-out section when that doc section exists. NO schema/recorder changes here - read-only consumer of z0k.3's API.

title: Armory graph: ingest br issue hierarchy from real project data
description: Closes the corpus-boundary gap left across 8e0.4 and t9o: OGENT_ISSUE_PARENT is now emitted by the bd Org projection (t9o) and honored by ogent-armory-build-graph (8e0.4), but no PRODUCTION path carries it between them - the projection file lives under user-emacs-directory (outside armory roots, by design per 9w7.3) and ogent-armory--issue-links only scans the armory root. Result: automated parent/child issue edges never actually reach a built graph. FIX (pick + record): (a) graph construction optionally ingests the project's .beads/issues.jsonl (or br show data) for issue-link records found in the root - resolving parent ids at build time from the export the DB already flushes; or (b) an armory-root-scoped issue projection (opt-in defcustom) written by the agenda projection alongside the cache copy. Option (a) preferred: no new writer, single source of truth, works for hand-authored issue-link records too. ACCEPTANCE (production-shaped, not synthetic): a test fixture using REAL br-export-shaped JSONL (issue_id/depends_on_id/type parent-child, exactly as .beads/issues.jsonl) plus an armory root containing issue-link records -> ogent-armory-build-graph emits issue:parent->issue:child 'parent edges; missing/malformed JSONL degrades silently; br list --json's dependency-less shape documented as insufficient (per t9o's helper docs).
close_reason: Graph now ingests real br hierarchy: build-graph reads the project's .beads/issues.jsonl (walk-up resolution) as parent fallback for issue-link records (OGENT_ISSUE_PARENT property wins when present), REAL export-shaped fixture proves issue:parent->issue:child edges end-to-end through ogent-armory-build-graph; malformed/missing JSONL degrades silently; cache stamp extended with the JSONL mtime+size and the regression provably fails under the old stamp (advice-verified). br list --json insufficiency documented. Armory 33/33, cache 5/5, status 19/19.

comments.author: jake
comments.text: DECIDED+IMPLEMENTED option (a): ogent-armory-build-graph now ingests the project's .beads/issues.jsonl (nearest export at or above the armory root, via ogent-armory-cache-issues-jsonl) to resolve parent ids for issue-link records lacking OGENT_ISSUE_PARENT; the record property still wins. Missing/malformed JSONL degrades silently (per-line condition-case). br list --json insufficiency documented in ogent-armory--issues-jsonl-parents docstring (cross-ref ogent-issues-bd--issue-parent). Cache freshness: ogent-armory-cache-stamp now covers the governing issues.jsonl mtime+size, so external br flushes invalidate the cached graph without FORCE; regression proves stale-cache failure under the old stamp. Tests: +3; armory suite 33/33 (30 baseline + 3), cache 5/5, status 19/19 green.
